message.content:
Explore the project at c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1 and give me a thorough overview. I need to understand:
1. What files exist (list all HTML, CSS, JS files)
2. Read the main HTML file completely
3. Read any CSS files related to the hero section
4. Read any JS files that control scroll behavior or video
5. How the hero section is structured - specifically around a video element and any text overlay

This is a luxury private aviation website. The hero has a video background. I need to understand exactly how it's built before planning modifications.

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough exploration of this project to understand the hero section structure, particularly the video background setup. Let me start by examining the project layout and then dive into the specific files.

1 import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react' 2 import { gsap, ScrollTrigger } from '@animations/gsap.config' 3 import useHeroProgress from '@store/useHeroProgress' 4 import { useReducedMotion } from '@hooks/useReducedMotion' 5 import { heroFrameManifest } from './frameManifest' 6 7 import FrameCanvas from './FrameCanvas' 8 import FrameLoader from './FrameLoader' 9 import HeroCopy from './HeroCopy' 10 import ScrollHint from './ScrollHint' 11 12 import styles from './HeroSection.module.css' 13 14 export default function HeroSection() { 15 const sectionRef = useRef(null) 16 const canvasApiRef = useRef(null) 17 const reduced = useReducedMotion() 18 19 useEffect(() => { 20 if (!sectionRef.current) return 21 if (reduced) { 22 useHeroProgress.setState({ progress: 1 }) 23 canvasApiRef.current?.setProgress(1) 24 return 25 } 26 27 const ctx = gsap.context(() => { 28 ScrollTrigger.create({ 29 trigger: sectionRef.current, 30 start: 'top top', 31 end: '+=400%', 32 pin: true, 33 scrub: 0.6, 34 invalidateOnRefresh: true, 35 onUpdate: (self) => { 36 const p = self.progress 37 useHeroProgress.setState({ progress: p }) 38 canvasApiRef.current?.setProgress(p) 39 }, 40 }) 41 }, sectionRef) 42 43 const refreshTimer = setTimeout(() => ScrollTrigger.refresh(), 80) 44 45 return () => { 46 clearTimeout(refreshTimer) 47 ctx.revert() 48 } 49 }, [reduced]) 50 51 return ( 52

1	import { forwardRef, useRef } from 'react'
2	import { useFrameSequence } from '@hooks/useFrameSequence'
3	import styles from './HeroSection.module.css'
4	
5	const FrameCanvas = forwardRef(function FrameCanvas({ manifest }, apiRef) {
6	  const canvasRef = useRef(null)
7	  useFrameSequence(canvasRef, manifest, apiRef)
8	  return <canvas ref={canvasRef} className={styles.canvas} aria-hidden="true" />
9	})
10	
11	export default FrameCanvas
12

1 import useHeroProgress from '@store/useHeroProgress' 2 import styles from './HeroSection.module.css' 3 4 export default function FrameLoader() { 5 const loaded = useHeroProgress((s) => s.framesLoaded) 6 const total = useHeroProgress((s) => s.framesTotal) 7 const ready = useHeroProgress((s) => s.ready) 8 9 const pct = total === 0 ? 0 : Math.round((loaded / total) * 100) 10 11 return ( 12

1 import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react' 2 import useHeroProgress from '@store/useHeroProgress' 3 import { readOverlayState } from '@animations/heroTimeline' 4 import styles from './HeroSection.module.css' 5 6 export default function HeroCopy() { 7 const heroRef = useRef(null) 8 const taglineRef = useRef(null) 9 const ctaRef = useRef(null) 10 11 useEffect(() => { 12 const apply = (p) => { 13 const { copy } = readOverlayState(p) 14 if (heroRef.current) heroRef.current.style.opacity = String(copy.heroOpacity) 15 if (taglineRef.current) taglineRef.current.style.opacity = String(copy.taglineOpacity) 16 if (ctaRef.current) ctaRef.current.style.opacity = String(copy.ctaOpacity) 17 } 18 let last = useHeroProgress.getState().progress 19 apply(last) 20 return useHeroProgress.subscribe((s) => { 21 if (s.progress === last) return 22 last = s.progress 23 apply(last) 24 }) 25 }, []) 26 27 return ( 28 <> 29

content.text: Now let me read the key animation and hook files that control the scroll behavior and frame rendering:

content.content:
1	import { useEffect, useRef, useImperativeHandle } from 'react'
2	import useHeroProgress from '@store/useHeroProgress'
3	
4	/**
5	 * Preloads frames and exposes a `setProgress(p)` method via the imperative
6	 * handle. Bypasses the store subscribe machinery for the canvas hot path,
7	 * because rAF + Zustand notify ordering was leaving the canvas stuck on the
8	 * first frame on some renders.
9	 */
10	export function useFrameSequence(canvasRef, manifest, apiRef) {
11	  const imagesRef = useRef([])
12	  const lastDrawnRef = useRef(-1)
13	  const rafRef = useRef(0)
14	  const pendingProgressRef = useRef(0)
15	
16	  // Preload + decode every frame, then mark ready in the store.
17	  useEffect(() => {
18	    if (!manifest || manifest.length === 0) return
19	    let cancelled = false
20	
21	    useHeroProgress.setState({
22	      framesTotal: manifest.length,
23	      framesLoaded: 0,
24	      ready: false,
25	    })
26	
27	    const images = new Array(manifest.length)
28	    let loaded = 0
29	
30	    const onSettled = (i, img) => {
31	      if (cancelled) return
32	      images[i] = img
33	      loaded += 1
34	      useHeroProgress.setState({ framesLoaded: loaded })
35	      if (loaded === manifest.length) {
36	        imagesRef.current = images
37	        useHeroProgress.setState({ ready: true })
38	        paint(pendingProgressRef.current, true)
39	      }
40	    }
41	
42	    manifest.forEach((src, i) => {
43	      const img = new Image()
44	      img.decoding = 'async'
45	      img.onload = () => onSettled(i, img)
46	      img.onerror = () => onSettled(i, img)
47	      img.src = src
48	    })
49	
50	    return () => {
51	      cancelled = true
52	    }
53	    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
54	  }, [manifest])
55	
56	  // Resize observer redraws current frame at new canvas dimensions.
57	  useEffect(() => {
58	    const canvas = canvasRef.current
59	    if (!canvas) return
60	    const ro = new ResizeObserver(() => {
61	      sizeCanvas(canvas)
62	      paint(pendingProgressRef.current, true)
63	    })
64	    ro.observe(canvas)
65	    sizeCanvas(canvas)
66	    return () => ro.disconnect()
67	    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
68	  }, [canvasRef])
69	
70	  // Imperative API: HeroSection's ScrollTrigger.onUpdate calls this directly.
71	  useImperativeHandle(
72	    apiRef,
73	    () => ({
74	      setProgress(p) {
75	        pendingProgressRef.current = p
76	        if (!rafRef.current) {
77	          rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
78	            rafRef.current = 0
79	            paint(pendingProgressRef.current)
80	          })
81	        }
82	      },
83	    }),
84	    [],
85	  )
86	
87	  function paint(progress, force = false) {
88	    const images = imagesRef.current
89	    const canvas = canvasRef.current
90	    if (!canvas || images.length === 0) return
91	    const idx = Math.max(
92	      0,
93	      Math.min(images.length - 1, Math.round(progress * (images.length - 1))),
94	    )
95	    if (!force && idx === lastDrawnRef.current) return
96	    const img = images[idx]
97	    if (!img || !img.complete || img.naturalWidth === 0) return
98	    const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d')
99	    ctx.imageSmoothingQuality = 'high'
100	    drawCover(ctx, img, canvas.width, canvas.height)
101	    lastDrawnRef.current = idx
102	  }
103	}
104	
105	function sizeCanvas(canvas) {
106	  const dpr = Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio || 1, 1.75)
107	  const { clientWidth, clientHeight } = canvas
108	  canvas.width = Math.floor(clientWidth * dpr)
109	  canvas.height = Math.floor(clientHeight * dpr)
110	}
111	
112	function drawCover(ctx, img, cw, ch) {
113	  const iw = img.naturalWidth || img.width
114	  const ih = img.naturalHeight || img.height
115	  if (!iw || !ih) return
116	  const canvasRatio = cw / ch
117	  const imgRatio = iw / ih
118	  let sx = 0
119	  let sy = 0
120	  let sw = iw
121	  let sh = ih
122	  if (imgRatio > canvasRatio) {
123	    sw = ih * canvasRatio
124	    sx = (iw - sw) / 2
125	  } else {
126	    sh = iw / canvasRatio
127	    sy = (ih - sh) / 2
128	  }
129	  ctx.drawImage(img, sx, sy, sw, sh, 0, 0, cw, ch)
130	}
131

1	/** Math helpers — kept tiny, no dependencies. */
2	const clamp01 = (v) => Math.min(1, Math.max(0, v))
3	const lerp = (a, b, t) => a + (b - a) * t
4	const easeOutCubic = (t) => 1 - Math.pow(1 - t, 3)
5	
6	/**
7	 * The pop-out / 3D screen-reveal effect is now baked directly into the video
8	 * (last ~2s of the cinematic = camera pulls back to reveal the cinema monitor).
9	 * The website's job is just to hold the canvas at the final frame and fade in
10	 * the brand copy + CTA over that revealed scene.
11	 *
12	 * Phase model (now driven entirely by the video, not by DOM state):
13	 *  0.00 – 0.10  Title fades up over the cabin opening
14	 *  0.10 – 0.20  Title fades out as camera passes through window
15	 *  0.30 – 0.45  Tagline fades in/out across the open-sky breathing beat
16	 *  0.85 – 1.00  Final reveal — CTA fades in over the popped-out screen frame
17	 */
18	export function readOverlayState(p) {
19	  // Hero copy: visible at the very start, gone before window dive.
20	  const heroOpacity =
21	    easeOutCubic(clamp01(p / 0.10)) * (1 - clamp01((p - 0.10) / 0.10))
22	
23	  // Tagline: floats in during the open-sky beat.
24	  const taglineOpacity = bell(p, 0.30, 0.45, 0.05)
25	
26	  // CTA: appears as the screen-reveal completes.
27	  const ctaOpacity = easeOutCubic(clamp01((p - 0.85) / 0.15))
28	
29	  // Scroll hint fades out almost immediately.
30	  const scrollHintOpacity = 1 - clamp01(p / 0.05)
31	
32	  return {
33	    copy: {
34	      heroOpacity: clamp01(heroOpacity),
35	      taglineOpacity,
36	      ctaOpacity,
37	    },
38	    scrollHintOpacity,
39	  }
40	}
41	
42	function bell(x, a, b, ramp) {
43	  if (x < a || x > b) return 0
44	  const fadeIn = clamp01((x - a) / ramp)
45	  const fadeOut = clamp01((b - x) / ramp)
46	  return Math.min(fadeIn, fadeOut)
47	}
48

1	import { create } from 'zustand'
2	
3	const useHeroProgress = create((set) => ({
4	  progress: 0,
5	  framesLoaded: 0,
6	  framesTotal: 0,
7	  ready: false,
8	  setProgress: (progress) => set({ progress }),
9	  setFramesLoaded: (framesLoaded) => set({ framesLoaded }),
10	  setFramesTotal: (framesTotal) => set({ framesTotal }),
11	  setReady: (ready) => set({ ready }),
12	}))
13	
14	export default useHeroProgress
15

1 import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react' 2 import useHeroProgress from '@store/useHeroProgress' 3 import { readOverlayState } from '@animations/heroTimeline' 4 import styles from './HeroSection.module.css' 5 6 export default function ScrollHint() { 7 const ref = useRef(null) 8 9 useEffect(() => { 10 const apply = (p) => { 11 const { scrollHintOpacity } = readOverlayState(p) 12 if (ref.current) ref.current.style.opacity = String(scrollHintOpacity) 13 } 14 let last = useHeroProgress.getState().progress 15 apply(last) 16 return useHeroProgress.subscribe((s) => { 17 if (s.progress === last) return 18 last = s.progress 19 apply(last) 20 }) 21 }, []) 22 23 return ( 24
25 Scroll 26
27
28 ) 29 } 30

1	import { gsap } from 'gsap'
2	import { ScrollTrigger } from 'gsap/ScrollTrigger'
3	
4	gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger)
5	
6	let lenisInstance = null
7	
8	export function syncWithLenis(lenis) {
9	  lenisInstance = lenis
10	
11	  lenis.on('scroll', ScrollTrigger.update)
12	
13	  gsap.ticker.add((time) => {
14	    lenis.raf(time * 1000)
15	  })
16	
17	  gsap.ticker.lagSmoothing(0)
18	}
19	
20	export function getLenis() {
21	  return lenisInstance
22	}
23	
24	export { gsap, ScrollTrigger }
25

1	import { useEffect } from 'react'
2	import Lenis from 'lenis'
3	import { syncWithLenis } from '@animations/gsap.config'
4	
5	export function useLenisScroll() {
6	  useEffect(() => {
7	    const lenis = new Lenis({
8	      duration: 1.4,
9	      easing: (t) => Math.min(1, 1.001 - Math.pow(2, -10 * t)),
10	      smoothWheel: true,
11	      touchMultiplier: 1.2,
12	    })
13	    syncWithLenis(lenis)
14	    return () => lenis.destroy()
15	  }, [])
16	}
17

content.text: Perfect! Now let me provide you with a thorough overview of the Aether project: ## Comprehensive Project Overview: AETHER Luxury Private Aviation Website ### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE & FILE INVENTORY **HTML/CSS/JS Files:** - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/index.html` - Root HTML entry point - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/main.jsx` - React root bootstrap - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/App.jsx` - Main app wrapper - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/components/sections/Hero/HeroSection.jsx` - Hero orchestrator - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/components/sections/Hero/FrameCanvas.jsx` - Canvas renderer - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/components/sections/Hero/HeroCopy.jsx` - Text overlay component - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/components/sections/Hero/ScrollHint.jsx` - Scroll indicator - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/components/sections/Hero/FrameLoader.jsx` - Loading progress UI - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/components/sections/Hero/frameManifest.js` - Frame sequence definition - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/components/sections/Hero/HeroSection.module.css` - Hero styling - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/styles/globals.css` - Global reset & Lenis config - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/styles/tokens.css` - Design system variables - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/animations/gsap.config.js` - GSAP + Lenis setup - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/animations/heroTimeline.js` - Overlay fade timings - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/hooks/useFrameSequence.js` - Frame preload + canvas rendering - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/hooks/useLenisScroll.js` - Smooth scroll initialization - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/hooks/useReducedMotion.js` - Accessibility hook - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/src/store/useHeroProgress.js` - Zustand state management **Media Assets:** - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/public/hero.mp4` - Source video (2560x1440, 30fps, 16MB) - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/public/frames/` - 274 desktop frames (frame_0001.webp through frame_0274.webp) - `c:\CLAUDE\Aether\aether-1/public/frames-mobile/` - 219 mobile frames (optimized for smaller devices) --- ### 2. HERO SECTION ARCHITECTURE #### Overall Layer Stack (Back to Front): ``` .hero (section, position: relative) ├── canvas (z-index: 1) — Frame rendering ├── .copyLayer (z-index: 4) — Brand copy + CTA ├── .scrollHint (z-index: 4) — Scroll indicator └── .loader (z-index: 10) — Loading spinner (front-most until frames preload) ``` #### How the Hero Section Works: **1.
